response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins by releasing histamines which can cause severe allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis which is life-threatening and not related to the environment of living underwater but rather to the presence of peanut allergens in their system  

Sec. 2  
living underwater does not eliminate the risk of exposure to peanut allergens as they may come into contact with contaminated water or food sources which can trigger their allergic response and the physiological effects of an allergic reaction are not influenced by the surrounding environment but by the presence of the allergen itself in their body
